success_surface: Text extraction formula implementation across multiple rows with proper reference updating and verification
effective_behaviors: Analyzed actual cell contents before implementing to understand the data pattern
Constructed Excel text extraction formula using MID/FIND functions for delimiter-based parsing
Applied formulas row-by-row with corrected cell references for each target row
Used openpyxl instead of pandas to preserve formula strings in cells
Verified post-save by reloading workbook to confirm formulas written correctly
evidence: Agent first examined spreadsheet content showing Row 1: 'PO 99171 Dell Vostro - Mark' → 'Dell Vostro' before designing formula
Created separate formulas for rows 1, 2, 3 with A1/A2/A3 references updated accordingly
Loaded workbook via load_workbook() and set .value directly (not pandas.to_excel)
Reloaded saved file and printed formula strings to confirm they remained as formulas not calculated values

memory_items.title: Exploratory Data Analysis Before Implementation
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect actual cell contents to identify patterns before designing extraction or transformation logic
memory_items.generalization: Read relevant rows/columns to sample data format, then derive the appropriate function/formula from observed patterns rather than assumptions

memory_items.title: Delimiter-Based Text Extraction Formulas
memory_items.lesson: For extracting substrings between delimiters (spaces, dashes, etc.), nest FIND/MID/LEFT/RIGHT functions appropriately
memory_items.generalization: Use FIND to locate delimiter positions, MID to extract range between positions, adjust offsets by counting occurrences of repeated delimiters

memory_items.title: Multi-Row Formula Application Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When applying formulas across multiple target cells, create distinct formula strings with updated cell references for each row
memory_items.generalization: Do not use single formula copied via loop without reference adjustment; explicitly generate formula list where source cell references increment per target row

memory_items.title: openpyxl for Formula Preservation
memory_items.lesson: openpyxl.write.value() preserves formula strings; pandas.to_excel converts formulas to static values
memory_items.generalization: Select tool based on whether formulas must remain editable formulas vs. become calculated values; use openpyxl Workbook.cells when formula retention is required

memory_items.title: Post-Save Formula Verification
memory_items.lesson: After saving, reload the workbook to verify formulas were written correctly as formulas not evaluated values
memory_items.generalization: Always read back formula-bearing cells post-write to confirm they contain formula strings beginning with '=' and match intended logic

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Text Extraction via Delimiter Functions
- For extracting text between characters (spaces, dashes, etc.), construct formulas using nested FIND + MID combinations.
- Count delimiter occurrences to determine start positions (e.g., second space requires FIND(...FIND(...)+1)).

### Multi-Row Formula Deployment
- When populating multiple rows with related formulas, generate a list where source cell references increment per row.
- Do not reuse identical formula strings across rows; update source column/row references explicitly.

### Formula Integrity Validation
- After writing formulas, reload the saved file to verify cells contain formula strings (=...) not pre-calculated values.
- Print formula content during verification to confirm structural correctness.
